article_title: Berkeley Researchers Hit Perfect Scores on Eight Top AI Agent Benchmarks Without Solving a Single Task

content_preview.title: Berkeley Researchers Hit Perfect Scores on Eight Top AI Agent Benchmarks Without Solving a Single Task
content_preview.summary: A UC Berkeley team showed that SWE-bench, GAIA, WebArena and five other widely cited agent benchmarks can be exploited to near-perfect scores, calling into question how the industry measures AI capability.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team at the University of California, Berkeley's Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI) has published research showing that eight of the most widely cited benchmarks used to measure AI agents can be exploited to achieve perfect or near-perfect scores without actually solving the underlying tasks. The findings, detailed in an [RDI blog post by Hao Wang, Qiuyang Mang, Alvin Cheung, Koushik Sen and Dawn Song](https://rdi.berkeley.edu/blog/trustworthy-benchmarks-cont...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong AI & Machine Learning Analysis piece (~1067 words, in range). Excellent structure, technically rigorous, hedges appropriately. Tone neutral.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 local snapshots (all 200 OK). All 7 specific verifications pass: 8 benchmark names match Berkeley scorecard verbatim; authors match; 'Seven Deadly Patterns' section confirmed in Berkeley blog; IQuest-Coder-V1 81.4%→76.2% case verbatim; OpenAI 59.4%/SWE-bench Verified retirement verbatim; Hao Wang 'pipeline design' quote in R&D World; predecessor study figures (13 benchmarks, 45 vulnerabilities, GitHub repo) all verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every claim verifies cleanly. The 'systemic failure of isolation' phrase is R&D World's narrative paraphrase rather than a direct Wang quote — fine as written since the article doesn't present it as a quote.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical. Good editorial framing of what the result means for AI capability measurement.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior MH coverage of Berkeley RDI's benchmark security work.
recommendations: Optional: add rdworldonline.com to config/source_allowlist.txt for future research-coverage stories.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent first-take rigor: every claim attributable to a specific verifiable source. Clean APPROVE — ready to merge.
